title: Chapter Forty-Three: Promotion
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The Seventh Apostle's one remaining eye was red. She stood twenty steps away, but her momentum approached like a blade pressed against skin.

Cheong Myeong, wearing his classical eyepatch, was handling five Blood Swordmasters alone some distance off. He probably had not heard.

Ma Jin rarely stammered.

"That -- what was that?"

"Demon Destroying Blue Steel Hand. A martial art to open the crowns of blood demons."

"You said you're Lightning Flash?" The Seventh Apostle smiled. Madness in it, formed smoothly and red. The pain that should have crippled her -- the agony that had made the first Blood Swordmaster Yeon-shin ever killed unable to think straight when a sword pierced his abdomen -- she had simply swallowed it whole.

"You're amazing. Where did you get such divine arts? Martial arts created assuming our sect's annihilation, and mastering it to that extent -- especially at that age."

"..."

"Even a little dharma power imbued in martial arts makes initiation difficult. If it were easy, would Shaolin have left us alone? Such techniques can only be cultivated by those born with once-in-a-millennium talent. You..."

The smile deepened. She looked completely like a madwoman.

"I want you. Your nape looks even prettier."

"Shut up!" Ma Jin roared.

The ground rumbled. He rushed in with momentum incomparable to before, overwhelming her. She had no more will to fight. Her body-protecting movement was like a bat's wing flap -- segmenting, darting, entering the realm of supreme ascension. She could deflect what came, but Ma Jin could not pin her down.

Yeon-shin was the same. Successfully striking the Seventh Apostle alone exceeded his ability. It had been a miracle -- created together by her carelessness, the Blood Swordmasters' assistance, and the dharma power's extreme compatibility.

He had already fulfilled his role. He had reversed a defeat.

"See you again," she said. Coquettish. Hair scattered long.

She smiled brightly and left the battlefield with speed Yeon-shin had never seen. She did not care about her remaining subordinates.

As Blood Swordmasters fell one after another, the surviving cultists knelt or committed suicide. The morale of a fanatical sect, broken.

Yeon-shin approached Ma Jin as the battlefield was cleaned up.

"The squad leader couldn't fulfil his role alone."

Ma Jin nodded slightly. "I know."

"In terms of martial power, you seemed half a level above the Seventh Apostle. Is my maternal family's body-protecting movement originally like that?"

"...I learned it well."

"I see."

"...Yes."

A place between uncle and nephew. Yeon-shin forced a smile. Perhaps due to anxiety from the initial failure, his peer-like manner had shown. It was the first time since entering Desolate Sect.

"This mission failed. Even if I fulfilled my role, the merit will be downgraded. Thanks to this I can push myself. I need to become stronger."

Won Jong approached while the corpses were being cleared. His reddened eyes contained deep emotion.

"You showed it. That martial arts of dharma power reaching the extreme actually existed..."

"It's thanks to you, master."

"The martial art that took the Seventh Apostle's eye, that divine force wave -- I won't forget until my dying moment. Wonderful talent and goodness."

*If I survive, I will take the Blood Flame Sect leader's head too.*

He hoped all the old monk's regrets would fade by then.

"If you ever come to Shaolin, I'll serve Longjing tea," Won Jong said, smiling broadly. "The abbot senior brother won't be able to hide his curiosity."

"Master uncle mentioned Longjing tea?" Gak Jeong stumbled over, eyes wide. "You stayed far from it like grain wine, calling it mere luxury."

"Your words are funny. Just because one is in Buddhism doesn't mean one should stay away from secular ways. Proper hospitality must be shown to honoured guests."

Gak Jeong sighed and looked at Yeon-shin. "I don't disagree. I've never seen someone like you. Just containing such martial arts in your body is amazing."

"I think I'll long remember the Little Divine Monk's dharma talk too."

Gak Jeong laughed loudly. A magnanimous monk.

"Come to Mount Song sometime. My master will welcome you."

"The Shaolin Temple abbot..."

The feeling of having an orthodox sect's absolute being in his mouth was strange. Yeon-shin had grown up in Henan, hearing Shaolin's reputation from birth.

He clasped his fists slowly. "Someday there will be a day to climb Mount Song. I'll be in your care then too."

"Amitabha."

Won Jong and Gak Jeong returned the greeting with banjang. Sunlight poured down amid thick blood scent.

Three of the Nine Sects. Yeon-shin had now formed connections with three.

---

The return journey was quieter than it should have been.

Hyeon Won-chang and Cheong Myeong took it upon themselves to tease Yeon-shin about the Righteous Heaven Sword Family's marriage offer. They had taken particular interest in discussing Lightning Flash's prospects.

*Fill your own seats first.*

The time came to part with the Shaolin monks. Won Jong earnestly requested he visit Shaolin. Gak Jeong could not let go.

"Amitabha. When there are endless dharma talks yet to hear..."

"It's already enough. I'll accept the monk's heart."

"You never know. Couldn't you sufficiently be called a rising talent? You might come to the Dragon Phoenix Gathering representing Desolate Sect."

"Did you say Dragon Phoenix Gathering?"

"A place where rising talents from orthodox martial sects interact. Many promising young martial artists gather to exchange moves and build friendships." Gak Jeong grinned. "Sometimes pairs form like mandarin ducks. Even an incomplete monk like me has his heart flutter."

*Trivial.*

Yeon-shin was not interested. Dark clouds hung over his blue-level promotion due to mission failure. He had no time for romance.

After bowing once more, the two monks departed.

The group rode through the ruined Xinye County. Yeon-shin passed by indifferently on horseback, recalling only two names.

The Tyrant Sword Sect leader. The Blood Flame Sect leader.

The heads he needed to take. Still distant absolute beings.

After several days, the group arrived in Xiangyang land. The majestic fortress of Desolate Sect appeared from afar, and Yeon-shin's heart became complex.

*Blue level.*

The first failure after three missions. He did not deny himself. He thought of continuous victories, believed if he endured the time of effort and hardship, he could reach it faster than anyone.

*Just doing well myself isn't enough. That's the murim.*

They entered Desolate Sect's main gate. Ma Jin went to report the mission. Yeon-shin was about to return with the seniors when a familiar face appeared.

The General Administration Office scholar, Do Yu-won.

"We've been waiting."

"Scholar Do...?"

Do Yu-won smiled. "Not just me. A white warrior taking a Blood Flame Sect apostle's eye -- the messenger bird's wings are more steady and swift than lightness technique. The news was widespread."

"Ah."

"You've reduced the fighting power of an apostle who could face black-level masters. That day, a schedule encompassing all of Desolate Sect was set." His voice carried the weight of ceremony. "Blue-level promotion. A place where all whites who have accumulated sufficient merit challenge. The summons was issued the moment you entered Xiangyang."

Not just Hyeon Won-chang but all the Radiant Demon Wing seniors followed. Their faces had completely erased the journey's fatigue. Something exciting had happened.

Yeon-shin approached Ma Jin's side and lightly touched his arm.

"My words were excessive last time."

Silence.

Meanwhile Baek Mi-ryeo muttered, "Not even giving time to rest?"

"It's the sect leader's will," Do Yu-won said.

*Consideration,* Yeon-shin thought. The scope of authority changed upon becoming blue-level. Cases of carrying out missions alone from the start.

*I'll accumulate merit without rest. I'm looking forward to it.*

They reached the central platform where a large tree grew alone. White feather clouds flowing in the clear sky. Dozens of white warriors had gathered. Many unfamiliar faces -- all competitors.

A central platform, a cypress tree three arms thick, and beneath its shade, reclining languidly against the brown trunk --

The Desolate Sect Lord.

Light grass-coloured ceremonial robes. Deep green pupils revealed through light green bangs as she opened her eyes.

Yeon-shin looked up at her. Their gazes tangled for an instant. He thought he saw her smile.

"From now," she said. Her voice brought silence. "I shall test your qualification for blue level."

Cheers erupted. Yeon-shin approached the centre and felt stern momentum from all directions -- blade-like energies directed at him from every white warrior present.

*Interesting.*

He spread five fingers and wrapped them around the Desolate Sword's scabbard. Jeong Family Dynamic Technique's energy arose. He began calmly receiving and deflecting all the energy flowing toward him.

Very light.

He felt it himself. He had already stepped into the realm of blue-level.

---
